reasons: There is no evidence of country-name canonicalization problems: diagnostics show zero country canonicalization events and no country near-mismatches.
There is no evidence of list-format inconsistencies: diagnostics report zero list-format events and observed_list_formats are empty across attributes.
Validation hints indicate scalar formatting for fields like platform, developer, publisher, and ESRB; they should not be treated as list columns.

The strongest normalization-sensitive issue mentioned in reasoning is semantic/text canonicalization for platform aliases (e.g. PC vs linux vs xbox 360), which can break exact comparisons and categorical survivorship.
Reasoning also recommends ESRB label canonicalization to a closed set, indicating text normalization could improve categorical consistency.
Reasoning recommends normalizing numeric-text handling for userScore and criticScore, but the evidence points more to fusion-rule/semantic issues than to case or list formatting.
normalization_directives.recommendation_summary: Rerun limited normalization focused on scalar text canonicalization for platform and ESRB, plus consistent userScore value formatting/parsing. Do not add country normalization or list normalization, since diagnostics show no evidence for those issues and validation style treats relevant fields as scalar. Keep id lowercase only, and preserve validation-style casing for other fields.

parameters.reason: Increase match thresholds to reduce over-matching and large clusters.

findings.recommendation: Tighten match thresholds and inspect ambiguous features for this pair.

findings.title: Gold/testset coverage is incomplete after id mapping
findings.recommendation: Improve id alignment or lineage preservation so evaluation records map reliably to fused entities.

findings.recommendation: Prioritize survivorship and coverage rules before fine-grained value canonicalization.

Module contents

PyDI: Python Data Integration Framework

This package provides tools for end‑to‑end data integration, including
schema matching, blocking, entity matching, information extraction,
normalization and validation, and data fusion. It is inspired by the WInte.r Java framework but takes a
pandas‑first approach. All modules aim to expose simple, composable functions
and classes with stable signatures and rich docstrings.
Subpackages

-----

evaluation_function
(
EvaluationFunction
) – Function to evaluate fusion results for this attribute. Typically
has signature
(fused_value,
expected_value,
**params)
->
bool
.
**kwargs
(
Any
) – Optional parameters to bind to the evaluation function (e.g.,
threshold=0.7
).
Return type
:
None
Examples
Register without parameters (exact signature):
>>> strategy.add_evaluation_function(“title”, tokenized_match)
Register with parameters using keyword args (will be bound via partial):
>>> strategy.add_evaluation_function(“director_name”, tokenized_match, threshold=0.7)
>>> strategy.add_evaluation_function(“actors”, tokenized_match, threshold=0.5)
